name: Raoden
description: Prince of Arelon, cursed by the Shaod and cast into Elantris. A natural leader who works to rebuild the fallen city from within.

name: Sarene
description: Princess of Teod, betrothed to Raoden. Sharp-witted, politically astute, and fiercely independent.

name: Hrathen
description: High Priest of Fjorden and Wyrn's gyorn, sent to convert Arelon to Shu-Dereth within three months.

name: Galladon
description: A Dula scholar from Duladen, Raoden's first companion in Elantris. Sardonic but deeply loyal.

name: Karata
description: Leader of one of the gangs within Elantris, former city guard. Fierce protector of the weak.

name: Shaor
description: Feral gang leader within Elantris, surrendered to the pain and madness of the Shaod.

name: Aanden
description: Leader of the most powerful gang in Elantris, obsessed with maintaining his former noble authority.

name: Dashe
description: Member of Karata's group in Elantris, loyal to her cause.

name: Dilaf
description: Hrathen's Arelish arteth, fanatical devotee of Jaddeth with a deep personal hatred of Elantris.

name: Roial
description: Duke of Sarene's circle, the wealthiest noble in Arelon and a shrewd political player.

name: Eondel
description: Count and military commander, loyal member of Sarene's opposition circle.

name: Iadon
description: King of Arelon, Raoden's father. A merchant king who rose to power after Elantris fell.

name: Kiin
description: Sarene's uncle, a powerful cook and former mercenary. Harbors many secrets.

name: Lukel
description: Kiin's son and Sarene's cousin, cheerful merchant and member of the inner circle.

name: Shuden
description: A JinDo nobleman, part of Sarene's political group, known for his grace and Jindo fighting style.

name: Telrii
description: Ambitious Arelish duke bribed by Hrathen to convert and destabilize Arelon's politics.

name: Omin
description: Priest of Shu-Korath, leader of the rival Derethi-opposed religion in Arelon.

name: Seinalan
description: Patriarch of Shu-Korath, Sarene's childhood friend, a high-ranking church official.

name: Eventeo
description: King of Teod, Sarene's father. A steady ruler devoted to his daughter's wellbeing.

name: Ashe
description: Sarene's seon, a sentient Aonic spirit who serves as her companion and messenger.

name: Rayse
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Odium, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Tanavast
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Honor, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Koravellium Avast
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Cultivation, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Ati
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Ruin, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Leras
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Preservation, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Aona
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Devotion, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Skai
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Dominion, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Edgli
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Endowment, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Bavadin
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Autonomy, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Uli Da
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Ambition, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Chan Ko Sar
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Invention, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Medelantorius
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Valor, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: Euridrius
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Reason, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering.

name: unknown
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Mercy, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering. Their identity is not yet known.

name: unknown
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Whimsy, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering. Their identity is not yet known.

name: unknown
description: Original Vessel of the Shard Virtuosity, one of the sixteen who took up a Shard of Adonalsium's power after the Shattering. Their identity is not yet known.

name: Hoid
description: One of the co-conspirators against Adonalsium during the Shattering. Did not take up a Shard.

name: Frost
description: Present at the Shattering. Did not take up a Shard; their role is otherwise unknown.

name: Sazed
description: A Terrisman Keeper who becomes the Vessel of Harmony after the Catacendre, holding the combined power of Ruin and Preservation. Spends time deciphering Kwaan's inscription, later discovering that Ruin was altering it, and falls in love with Tindwyl. Specializes in religion, and loses faith in it after Tindwyl's death. As Harmony, later finds himself in conflict with the Shard Autonomy.

name: Vin
description: A Mistborn discovered by Marsh and Kelsier at age sixteen. Trained in Allomancy by Kelsier, she learns quickly and is notable for an exceptionally strong ability to burn bronze, including the ability to pierce copperclouds. Often bothered by the lingering voice of her brother, Reen, who taught her how to survive on the streets, she uses her street smarts to manipulate the nobility and the underground alike. Becomes romantically involved with Elend Venture while disguised as a noblewoman. Struggles with her love of Elend while protecting him from enemy Allomancers, fighting with Zane until finally choosing to believe she is the Hero of Ages; finds the Well of Ascension and lets the powers contained there dissipate, releasing Ruin. A fully fledged Mistborn and very powerful Allomancer, wife of Elend Venture, thought to be the Hero of Ages until recently. Also a Sliver of Preservation later in the book.

name: Kelsier
description: A Mistborn and skilled thief, and a visionary whose seemingly impossible plans often succeed. Sent to the Pits of Hathsin, an atium mine where the most dangerous criminals are sent for the remainder of their lives, he is the only known survivor, earning him the name "Survivor." He has long horizontal scars on his wrists from digging in the pits. He gathers a group of Mistings intent on stealing the Lord Ruler's cache of atium and leading the skaa in revolt. Skilled with all metals, especially steel (Pushing) and iron (Pulling). Rescues Vin from the streets. His former wife, Mare, died in the Pits of Hathsin after an assumed betrayal, and he struggles to balance grief over losing her with anger at her betrayal. Killed by the Lord Ruler in the Final Empire, but manages to stay behind as a "ghost" (cognitive shadow) and later binds his spirit to a body. Leads the Ghostbloods.

name: Elend Venture
description: Son of Straff Venture, a nobleman with high ideals intent on a new form of democratic government. Born into one of the most powerful noble houses, but uninterested in the Final Empire's monarchical system. An intellectual who loves to read, including books such as the Book of the False Dawn that criticize the Lord Ruler. Attempts to rule his new kingdom fairly by the constitution he created, but is eventually deposed. Becomes Emperor of the Central and Northern Dominances after the Lord Ruler's fall, and a powerful Allomancer after ingesting a bead of Lerasium at the Well of Ascension. Husband of Vin. Eventually killed by Marsh at Ruin's command.

name: Breeze
description: A very skilled Soother, believed to be one of the more powerful Soothers in the Final Empire, who Soothes the emotions of everyone around him, often without thought. A nobleman, though he rarely reveals his background publicly. Contributes to Kelsier's crew through subtle jobs like Soothing people's emotions. Masquerades as a skaa, and establishes his own noble house after Elend becomes Emperor; it is assumed that Waxillium Ladrian is his descendant.

name: Spook
description: A young Tineye who speaks often in street slang and is sometimes incomprehensible. His actual name is Lestibournes, street slang for "I've been abandoned." Clubs's nephew. Had a romantic attraction to Vin for a short time. Becomes a Tineye Savant working as a spy in Urteau, where he inadvertently gains the title "Survivor of the Flames" and instigates a rebellion against the city's government. Made into a Mistborn by Sazed.

name: Marsh
description: Kelsier's brother, also a Seeker. Infiltrates the Steel Ministry, but is turned into a Steel Inquisitor through Hemalurgy. Forced to do Ruin's will while still retaining all his mental faculties; eventually kills Elend at Ruin's command. Later known as Ironeyes, or Death, a mythical figure associated with death; his life depends on his all-but-gone Atium stash, without which his life would end.

name: Hammond
description: A Pewterarm, or Thug, in Kelsier's gang. An excellent soldier, strong even without burning pewter. Unlike most Pewterarms, he loves to discuss philosophy. Later serves as a General in Emperor Elend's army.

name: Clubs
description: The Smoker of Kelsier's crew, able to burn copper to hide Allomancers around him. The crew uses his shop as a secret base of operations in Luthadel. Usually grim and pessimistic.

name: OreSeur
description: Impersonating Lord Renoux, OreSeur is a mysterious kandra who has a contract with Kelsier.

name: TenSoon
description: A kandra of the Third Generation, contracted to Zane and ordered to impersonate OreSeur so he could spy on Vin. After Zane is killed by Vin, becomes contracted to her instead. Returns to his kandra homeland only to be imprisoned there, later escaping and encountering Sazed, who he takes back to the kandra homeland with him.

name: Tindwyl
description: A Keeper whose specialty of study is leadership. Comes to Luthadel at Sazed's request to train Elend to be a king. After Elend is deposed, devotes her time to deciphering Kwaan's inscription with Sazed. Has died by the time of the Hero of Ages, a loss that leaves Sazed having lost faith in religion.

name: Zane
description: A Mistborn sent from one of the other armies. Later revealed to have been sent by Straff Venture, and to be Straff's son.

name: Straff Venture
description: Elend's father, king of the northern dominance and leader of the first army to arrive at Luthadel.

name: Ashweather Cett
description: King of the western dominance and leader of the second army to arrive at Luthadel. Later serves as an advisor to Emperor Elend Venture.

name: Aradan Yomen
description: Lord and king of Fadrex City. Elend's biggest rival, who even manages to capture Vin for a short while.

name: Human
description: A koloss who talks to Vin and reveals the origins of his species.

name: Quellion
description: Also known as the Citizen. A man who took over control of Urteau after the Lord Ruler's demise.

name: Waxillium Ladrian
description: The descendant of Breeze and an extremely powerful Twinborn. Returns to his estates in Elendel, after spending most of his life in the Roughs, intent on assuming his duties to his house until a new threat emerges and pulls him into the fray. The hereditary Lord of House Ladrian and a former lawman in the Roughs, he holds a special deputized forbearance in the constabulary. A Twinborn with the Allomantic ability to Push metals and the Feruchemical ability to increase or decrease his weight. Later becomes a special constable and detective, and a senator.

name: Wayne
description: Wax's smart-mouthed, talented partner and long-time friend. Also a Twinborn, but fights with dueling canes and speed bubbles rather than guns. Has a knack for impersonation and a penchant for "trading" items in others' possession with something of his own. His Allomantic ability makes speed bubbles, in which time speeds up inside and slows down outside; his Feruchemical ability stores health in goldminds to heal quickly, in exchange for a later period of poor health. Later a constable in Elendel law enforcement.

name: Marasi Colms
description: The "cousin" of Steris Harms who is actually the illegitimate daughter of Lord Harms and, therefore, half-sister to Steris. Studies legal justice and criminal behaviouristics, and is particularly interested in Wax and Wayne's exploits in the Roughs. A constable and aide to Constable-General Claude Aradel in the Fourth Octant, serving as an analyst and his executive assistant. A Pulser, an Allomancer who can slow down time within a bubble and speed up time outside it; later uses her ability via grenades that stop time when thrown.

name: Miles Dagouter
description: Also known as Miles "Hundredlives." Once an associate of Wax, his ideals have driven him to lead the Vanishers and become entwined in the plans of the Set. A Twinborn, able to compound gold and appear as if immortal, hence his nickname.

name: Steris Harms
description: The daughter of Lord Harms and Wax's fiancee. Stern and business-like, showing a distaste for most of Wax's personality as she holds the image society has determined to be of the utmost importance. Kidnapped by Miles; Wax, Wayne, and Marasi manage to rescue her. Has a reputation of being a bore. Later becomes Wax's wife, with whom she has two children; Marasi's half-sister.

name: Tarson
description: One of Miles' lieutenants in the Vanishers. Of koloss blood and a Pewter Allomancer, making for an extremely deadly combination of physical strength. At the Yomen party he tries to kidnap Marasi but is stopped by Wax; after the Breaknaught is stolen, Wax shoots him in the head, killing him.

name: Ranette
description: A marvellous gunsmith who harbours a sour feeling for both Wax and Wayne, almost shooting them when they visit her. After being given an aluminum gun, lends Wax her newest gun, Vindication, specially designed to be used only by Coinshots or Lurchers.

name: Edwarn Ladrian
description: Wax's presumed dead uncle, masquerading as Mister Suit, the mysterious benefactor of the Vanishers and a member of a powerful aristocratic secret society known only as the Set. His motives are revealed when Wax determines him to still be alive and confronts him. One of the Set's leaders.

name: Jackstom Harms
description: The father of Steris, and later revealed to also be the father of Marasi by a different woman. Accompanies Steris to her liaisons with Wax before she is kidnapped, and is willing to financially back Wax in his investigation of the Vanishers.

name: Paalm
description: Also known as Bleeder. A rogue and deranged Kandra of the Third Generation, who once served the Lord Ruler. Plots to block Harmony's influence over Elendel.

name: MeLaan
description: A Kandra of the Seventh Generation, previously appearing in the original Mistborn trilogy. Sent by Harmony to aid Waxillium in incapacitating Bleeder.

name: Replar Innate
description: Governor of Elendel, whom Marasi believes to be corrupt. His brother Winsting Innate was recently murdered at a meeting with various criminal factions.

name: Claude Aradel
description: Constable-General of the Fourth Octant, a man of honor who has not a single drop of noble blood in him.

name: Telsin Ladrian
description: Wax's sister and the leader of the Set, a terrorist group. Becomes the avatar of the Shard Autonomy.
